criteria_checked.note: r1 B1 is RESOLVED and empirically verified GREEN. Rev 2's inventory-last order [1,2,3,6,7,8,9,10,4,5] PRESERVES the exact §6->§7 and §9->§10 physical adjacencies that V1 (:683) and V4 (:697-698) depend on, so under this order V1 correctly deletes §6 (-> REFUSED missing 6), V4 empties ONLY §9 keeping all 10 sections (the r1 collateral-delete of §4/§5/§6/§8 is GONE), and test-agents-md.sh:182 (§6->§7 awk) still deletes §6 -> validate REFUSES. Simulated all fixtures over a real generated doc reordered to [1,2,3,6,7,8,9,10,4,5]: V1 PASS, V4 PASS (sections present [1..10]), sh:182 PASS. The in-scope rewrite to emitted-order-independent construction (T001) is a correct, harmless hardening on top. B1 does not recur.

NON-BLOCKING GAP. The plan's rewrite scope names V1/V4/awk:182, but the reorder's ACTUAL changed adjacencies are §3->§6 (was §3->§4) and §10->§4 (was §10-terminal) — and TWO order-dependent fixtures keyed on §10-being-terminal are NOT in the plan's rewrite list and are wrongly certified 'order-independent' by the plan's Finding (lines 92-94, which lists E1/E3 but omits E4): (1) E4 module selftest dmc-agents-md.py:658 `'package manager' in empty_doc.split('## 10.')[-1]`; (2) test-agents-md.sh:144 `awk '/^## 10\./{f=1} f' | grep 'package manager'`. Under inventory-last their slice now spans [4,5,10] instead of just §10. BOTH still PASS (empirically confirmed) because 'package manager' appears ONLY in §10's body for the empty fixture (§4='- Unknown', §5=secret bullets/bindings, neither contains the token) — so they still isolate §10 in practice and module selftest stays 0 FAIL. Non-blocking (no AC fails, goal achieved), but the plan's completeness claim is inaccurate. RECOMMEND folding E4 and sh:144 into the same emitted-order-independent rewrite (bound the §10 slice by §10's own heading -> next-emitted-heading, per the plan's own stated principle) and correcting the Finding. Advisory, not a gate.

The stated rewrite ('locate a section by its own heading + the immediately-following EMITTED heading via split_sections/HEADING_RE offsets, never a hardcoded ## N. successor') is sound and implementable and keeps each negative control testing its INTENDED defect (V1 -> missing §6; V4 -> empty §9; awk -> missing §6), not a no-op: deleting/emptying a section by its own [heading, next-emitted-heading) span is exactly the defect each control needs. IMPLEMENTATION NOTE (non-blocking): a shared helper must handle the LAST-emitted-section case (no following heading -> run to EOF); under inventory-last §5 is last, but none of V1/V4/awk target §5, so the three named controls are safe. If E4/sh:144 are folded in (recommended above), the §10 lookup returns §4 as the next emitted heading — bound the slice there.

The count-parity guard is correctly de-tautologized. §5's protected set is a filtered subset of §4 BY CONSTRUCTION (:215-218), so the r1 subset check was a tautology; a per-class COUNT equality (rendered §5 counts == derived §4 per-class counts) instead validates the RENDER. Well-defined: each landmark carries exactly one class, so enforcement/contract/release counts partition the union with no overlap. NON-BLOCKING IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: the guard must PARSE the RENDERED §5 string (e.g. regex the emitted 'N enforcement / M contract / K release') and compare to a freshly-derived §4 per-class count — if it instead recomputes BOTH sides from facts['landmarks'] it re-tautologizes and guards nothing. The compact §5 render also newly requires per-class counts (currently only the union set `protected_landmarks` exists) — that derivation is in-scope in the §5 render edit.

No RUNTIME consumer or gate keys on §4/§5 POSITION. Confirmed (r1 + re-grep): repo-intel.py:213 (filename tuple), context-budgeter:49,155 (filename + static lines:60 estimate), doctor.py:225 (os.path.isfile), v0.1.3-verify.sh:47 (existence), context-audit AC6 (content GREP for AUTONOMY.md/CONTEXT_MAP.md in §7 — position-independent), context-audit AC4 (wc -l bloat bound 400 — position-independent; post-dedup ~286 lines). No tool HASHES AGENTS.md or checks section ORDER. The only position-dependent constructs are the two TEST fixtures E4/sh:144 (criterion a). §4->§5 adjacency is preserved (both relocate together, order [...,4,5]) so test-agents-md.sh:140-142 (§4..§5 awk) is unaffected — verified PASS.

Goal achieved and ACs machine-checkable: §7<§4 and §9<§4 hold under inventory-last (simulated offsets §7=629, §9=1630, §4=2848). Byte margin: removing the confirmed 8,489 B §5 blob (AGENTS.md:226) takes 32,490 -> ~24,001 B < 28,672. VALID (validator order-independent, dict-keyed by number). 802/3/3 unaffected (generator not among the 49 dmc-v0.* legacy tools; mirror-check set is dmc-v0.* only). DEFAULT_PROTECTED override correctly dropped (generator not in the set); non-degrading landmark FLAG still expected. Bucket-A holds (no floor/gate/when-gates-fire/schema).

ADVISORY (non-blocking). Inventory-last places §5 (Protected surfaces / secret-pattern reminder) DEAD LAST, after the unbounded §4 inventory. On a host where §4 alone exceeds project_doc_max_bytes, §5 is the FIRST casualty of host truncation. This degrades only a defense-in-depth DOC reminder, NOT the enforcement floor (real secret protection is secret-guard.sh / pre-tool-guard.sh / instruction-level CLAUDE.md+DMC.md, never AGENTS.md §5), and the generator still emits its oversize_warning. For THIS repo (post-dedup ~24 KB < 32,768) no truncation occurs. CONSIDER ordering §5 BEFORE §4 (e.g. [1,2,3,5,6,7,8,9,10,4]) so the secret reminder survives truncation while still satisfying §7<§4/§9<§4 — or accept and document. Also NOTE two stale 'subset-parity' references the Rev 2 body did not update: Relevant Files row (plan line 108) and Verification Commands row (line 234) still say 'subset-parity' where the guard is now COUNT-parity. Cosmetic.
